Residual Damage: Hitmontop is worn down quickly by Spikes and Toxic Spikes because it doesn't hold Heavy-Duty Boots and almost never runs any recovery. If Hitmontop loses its Protective Pads, it's liable to being worn down by Rocky Helmet users like Mesprit, which carries Knock Off.

Whimsicott and Tera Fairy Users: Although Hitmontop has Bullet Punch to threaten many Fairy-types, particularly the frail Whimsicott, it's often not strong enough to OHKO them. Whimsicott outspeeds Hitmontop, resists Mach Punch, and can threaten an OHKO with Moonblast if it's holding a Choice Specs. Similarly, Tera Fairy users like Magneton and Jolteon can use the offensive and defensive benefits of the Fairy typing to improve their matchups against Hitmontop.

Flying-types: If Hitmontop can't outspeed Flying-types and take them out with Triple Axel, then it can be left in a vulnerable position due to only having a potentially resisted priority move. Charizard can neuter Hitmontop with a burn from Will-O-Wisp if it doesn't just take it out with STAB Hurricane. Drifblim can spinblock against Hitmontop and threaten to burn it, but it takes a substantial amount of damage from Triple Axel.

Sableye: Hitmontop struggles to use Rapid Spin or even Close Combat if Sableye can switch in and lock it into these moves with Encore, and Sableye can also threaten to burn Hitmontop.

Rapid Spin.description:
Hitmontop is a solid offensive entry hazard remover for its uncommon access to Rapid Spin, solid matchups against spinblockers such as Spiritomb and Trevenant, and high threat level from Close Combat and Technician-boosted Triple Axel. Triple Axel threatens checks to Hitmonotp like Whimsicott and Trevenant, and it also pressures Ice-weak physically defensive Pokemon like Dipplin and Sandslash. Bullet Punch and Mach Punch are important offensive utility to make up for Hitmontop's middling Speed, finishing off weakened sweepers and threatening faster Pokemon. Bullet Punch, despite not benefitting from STAB, is preferred over Mach Punch for its stronger matchups against Whimsicott, Tera Fairy Jolteon, and Hisuian Sneasel. However, Mach Punch is still a fine option and distinguishes itself by improving Hitmontop's potential to pick off Fighting-weak Pokemon like Sneasel, Lycanroc, and Beartic, as well as Basculin. All of Triple Axel, Bullet Punch, and Mach Punch benefit from Hitmontop's Technician powering them up, giving Hitmontop enough power to generally OHKO weakened Minior with Bullet Punch and assuredly OHKO offensive Venusaur with Triple Axel. As Hitmontop's moves make contact and it often runs into Rocky Helmet users like Mesprit and Weezing, Hitmontop should run Protective Pads to worry less about taking massive residual damage by using Triple Axel. Running 176 Speed EVs lets Hitmontop outspeed neutral-natured variants of Abomasnow, Clawitzer, and Farigiraf, and +1 Hitmontop will outspeed Charizard. With Tera Steel, Hitmontop can better shrug off Psychic-, Fairy-, and Flying-type attacks from special attackers like Mesprit, Whimsicott, and Minior; it also boosts Bullet Punch.

As an offensive spinner, Hitmontop's entry hazard removal is appreciated by more offensive teams that want to keep hazards off for threats such as non-Heavy-Duty Boots Jolteon, Clawitzer, Sneasel, Whimsicott, and Basculin. Similarly, Hitmontop is a good fit as the hazard remover for Sticky Web teams anchored by Vikavolt, which can check Mesprit and Weezing. As Hitmontop can be quite threatening against walls like Regirock and Cryogonal if it's given a free switch against them, it pairs nicely with pivots like Vikavolt, Jolteon, and Mesprit that tend to draw them in. Whimsicott can put Hitmontop on the back foot by outspeeding it and threatening it with STAB Moonblast, so Hitmontop should be paired with teammates like Muk, Articuno, and Charizard that can switch into Whimsicott until it's safely in range of being KOed by Bullet Punch. Mesprit is another threat that can bother Hitmontop, making Steel-types that answer it, such as Perrserker and Magneton, good partners. Weezing is a major pain for Hitmontop, making switch-ins to it, such as Mesprit, important.

Sableye.comments:
Other Options

Foul Play can be run over Knock Off to further punish setup, especially against Dark-types that would otherwise freely set up on Sableye such as Sneasel and Tera Dark Toxicroak. Taunt can ruin opposing defensive Pokemon like Lanturn and Venusaur, as well as deny entry hazards from being set, but Encore is better at handling offensive threats. Calm Mind sets may seem tempting with priority recovery, but Sableye's poor overall stats leaves it outclassed in this role by Spiritomb.

Checks and Counters

Dark-types: Dark-types are immune to Prankster and resist Sableye's attacks, meaning they can easily switch into it. Spiritomb, Skunktank and Sneasel turns Sableye into setup fodder, Brute Bonnet with Booster Energy doesn't care about Knock Off, and Houndoom can grab a Flash Fire boost from Will-O-Wisp. Hisuian Sneasel and Toxicroak can flip around their poor matchup to Sableye with Terastallization.

Special Attackers: Special wallbreakers like Jolteon, Arboliva, and Clawitzer don't mind Will-O-Wisp and can capitalize on Sableye's poor special bulk. Whimsicott in particular can OHKO Sableye with its STAB Moonblast.

Priority Blocking: While rare, priority blocking from Indeedee-F's Psychic Surge and Farigaraf's Armor Tail completely shut down Sableye's status moves.

Physical Wall.description:
Prankster-boosted status moves and an excellent matchup into top tiers like Mesprit make Sableye well-suited to be a physical wall in ZU. Encore allows Sableye to shut down opposing offensive Pokemon, stifling setup attempts from Minior, Toxicroak, and more. Will-O-Wisp neuters physical attackers, while Thunder Wave slows down foes and deters special attackers like Whimsicott from switching into Sableye. Knock Off removes crucial items like Leftovers and Heavy-Duty Boots, giving Sableye an easily spammable move that ensures progress. Heavy-Duty Boots lets Sableye switch in and out of battle without fearing entry hazard chip damage, while Rocky Helmet further punishes physical attackers and deters U-turn from the likes of Perrserker and Mesprit. Tera Poison gives Sableye a Fairy resistance while retaining a good matchup into opposing Fighting-types and giving it a Toxic immunity.

Sableye's ability to deny opposing setup paired with its excellent defensive typing makes it a great glue piece to round out a balance or bulky offense team. Its myriad immunities deter Fighting-types like Hitmontop and Psychic-types like Mesprit from using their STAB attacks. Special walls like Lanturn and Muk enjoy having Sableye to fall back on against physical threats, while Sableye appreciates their ability to handle special wallbreakers like Jolteon and Whimsicott. Dark-types like Spiritomb, Brute Bonnet, and Skunktank, as well as Tera Dark users like Hisuian Sneasel and Toxicroak are immune to Sableye's Prankster moves, easily using it as an entry point. As such, checks to these special threats like Whimsicott and Hitmontop are crucial teammates for Sableye. Entry hazard setters like Regirock, Orthworm, and Whiscash have great synergy with Sableye, as it naturally blocks Rapid Spin and can remove Heavy-Duty Boots with Knock Off. Sableye is also a good Knock Off absorber itself, which helps these Leftovers-reliant Pokemon.

Whiscash.comments:
Other Options

Earthquake can be run over Earth Power to hit Calm Mind Jolteon harder. Hydro Pump is an alternative Water-type STAB move that reduces passivity and is stronger than Surf, but is unreliable.

Checks and Counters

Grass-types and Grass-type coverage: Foes like Arboliva, Dipplin, Brute Bonnet, Venusaur, and Whimsicott completely wall both of Whiscash's STAB moves and threaten it back with their STAB moves. However, Dipplin and Whimsicott should be wary of Ice Beam on switch-in. Pokemon with Grass-type coverage like Energy Ball Vikavolt are typically able to KO Whiscash even from full health, either forcing it to switch out or Terastallize.

Wallbreakers: Wallbreakers like Clawitzer, Nasty Plot Mesprit, Glastrier, and Spiritomb all deal with Whiscash well because of its passive nature, and Mesprit and Spiritomb can freely set up against it.

Hazard Removal: Cryogonal and Hitmontop can force Whiscash out or to Terastallize. However, Cryogonal is threatened by it if it carries Stone Edge. Drifblim can take Whiscash's moves comfortably and whittle it down with Will-O-Wisp and Hex or Shadow Ball.

Entry Hazard Setter.description:
Leveraging its excellent typing, respectable bulk, and access to both Stealth Rock and Spikes, Whiscash is one of the tier's premier entry hazard setters. While Stealth Rock is preferred if the team's sole entry hazard setter is Whiscash, Spikes can be used to hit Pokemon that resist Stealth Rock like Sandslash, Magneton, and Orthworm harder, and Whiscash can run both Stealth Rock and Spikes on the same set for role compression. Whiscash's final moveslot can be either Ice Beam or Stone Edge; Ice Beam lets it hit switch-ins like Dipplin, Whimsicott, and Alolan Exeggutor hard, which would usually resist Whiscash's STAB moves. Stone Edge can be run over Ice Beam to hit Cryogonal hard, which would shake off Ice Beam and Surf due to its excellent Special Defense. However, Whiscash would only opt for Stone Edge if it chooses to run Spikes. Heavy-Duty Boots allows it to reliably come in multiple times to set hazards and block Volt Switch from the likes of Magneton and Jolteon more often without the fear of taking entry hazard chip damage, while Leftovers provides more longevity. Whiscash can choose to run either a physically defensive EV spread or a specially defensive one; the physically defensive spread gives Whiscash a more reliable way to withstand hits from Hitmontop, Sandslash, and Glastrier, while the specially defensive one allows Whiscash to check other special attackers such as Jolteon, Typhlosion, and Charizard. With Tera Ghost, Whiscash can block Rapid Spin from Hitmontop, Sandslash, and Cryogonal. Tera Poison flips its Grass weakness to have an easier time dealing with Arboliva and Virizion, while still keeping a solid defensive typing.

Whiscash fits on a myriad of teams that are looking to have an entry hazard setter that has both Stealth Rock and Spikes. Whiscash appreciates a spinblocker like Spiritomb or Sableye to keep entry hazards up at all times. Setup sweepers such as Jolteon, Mesprit, and Sneasel admire Whiscash's ability to reliably dent entry hazard-weak Pokemon like Venusaur, Magneton, and Regirock for them to grasp KOs more easily. Whiscash benefits from teammates like Charizard, Magneton, Cryogonal, and Muk, as they can handle Grass-types such as Whimsicott, Arboliva, and Venusaur. Whiscash also appreciates Knock Off users such as Mesprit, Skuntank, and Sableye, which complement its entry hazards by removing Heavy-Duty Boots from Pokemon such as Drifblim and Sneasel, allowing them to be worn down more easily. Whiscash makes for an excellent partner for Pokemon vulnerable to Poison-types and Fire-types, such as Whimsicott, Arboliva, Magneton, and Glastrier.

Regirock.comments:
Other Options

On paper, Sturdy gives a healthy Regirock a guaranteed turn to set up Stealth Rock, but between Regirock being so bulky and entry hazards being ubiquitous, it's unlikely that Regirock would be at risk of being OHKOed from full HP outside of uncommonly strong hits like Clawitzer's Water Pulse or Alolan Exeggutor's Leaf Storm. Regirock is totally dependent on the passive recovery from Leftovers to be a reliable wall, so even items like Heavy-Duty Boots and Rocky Helmet are suboptimal. Stone Edge is generally stronger than Rock Blast but has unreliable accuracy and poor PP, and it and Rock Slide cannot break past Substitute Pokemon. Curse can be used as a setup move over Iron Defense to power up Regirock's Rock Blast, but the extra Defense from Iron Defense is more useful to stonewall physical attackers, and Regirock's Body Press is more powerful and reliable than its STAB move anyway. Protect can enhance Regirock's recovery from Leftovers and allow Regirock to scout common Choice item attackers like Jolteon and Whimsicott, but it makes Regirock much more passive. Tera Ghost lets Regirock become immune to Fighting-type moves from major offensive threats like Virizion and Primeape while enabling it to block Rapid Spin from Sandslash and Toedscruel. Tera Grass helps Regirock manage its common Grass, Water, and Ground weaknesses, whereas Tera Water lets Regirock better handle Glastrier, and Tera Steel shares Tera Poison's poison immunity and Grass resistance while giving Regirock an easier time against Mesprit and Farigiraf. Regirock can run a Careful nature to patch up its relatively lower special bulk, but Regirock is often tasked with taking on physical attackers, and more Defense investment benefits its IronPress combination.

Checks and Counters

Ground-types: Sandaconda has great longevity, while Sandslash and Toedscruel can remove Regirock's entry hazards with Rapid Spin, but all three must be wary of Regirock boosting too much with Iron Defense.

Special Attackers: Regirock's lower special bulk leaves it prone to being overwhelmed by special attackers like Mesprit, Jolteon, and Rotom. Magneton can hit Regirock super effectively with its STAB Flash Cannon. However, most special attackers are physically frail, and Magneton takes a lot of damage from Regirock's Body Press. Many special attackers also cannot KO Regirock without significant chip or several boosts.

Fighting-types: Regirock's Fighting weakness can lead it to fall victim to Fighting-types like Primeape and Virizion. Hitmontop and Hitmonchan can both remove Regirock's Stealth Rock. However, Regirock can turn the tables on physically offensive Fighting-types with its IronPress combination and Terastallization.

Grass-types: Whimsicott, Alolan Exeggutor, and Sceptile can blow through Regirock with their strong super effective STAB moves, and Whimsicott is distinguished for avoiding a 2HKO from Regirock's Body Press after Stealth Rock. Alolan Exeggutor annihilates Regirock, while Dipplin can phaze Regirock with Dragon Tail and use Recover to wall it back.

Water-types: Poliwrath can use non-IronPress Regirock as setup fodder. Clawitzer can OHKO Regirock with Water Pulse but cannot switch into Regirock's Body Press more than once with Stealth Rock up. Veluza resists Body Press and can break through Regirock's Defense boosts with Aqua Cutter.

Knock Off, Trick, and Switcheroo: Regirock relies on its Leftovers for recovery, so Knock Off users like Sneasel and Mesprit are disruptive to Regirock. Mespirit and Hisuian Sneasel can use Trick and Switcheroo to disrupt Regirock by giving it their Choice items.

Residual damage: Burns and poison neutralize Regirock's already limited passive recovery. Burns from Will-O-Wisp users like Weezing and Rotom halve Regirock's damage output, while Toxic from Muk and Toedscruel puts Regirock on a timer. Entry hazards like Stealth Rock and Spikes cause Regirock to struggle to stay healthy.

Taunt and Encore: Encore users like Whimsicott and Mesprit can lock Regirock into a move to exploit it though neither enjoy taking Body Press. Froslass and Primeape can use Taunt to stop Regirock from setting up Stealth Rock or Iron Defense. Sableye can run Taunt or Encore and does not fear Regirock's Body Press.

Physical Wall.description:
Thanks to its titanic Defense, Regirock can stonewall popular physical attackers like Sneasel and use them as an opportunity to set up Stealth Rock. Rock Blast deals reliable damage to Typhlosion and Charizard and breaks Substitute on users like Minior and Jolteon. Body Press crushes Ice-types like Glastrier and Sneasel, and Steel-types like Orthworm and Magneton. Iron Defense boosts Regirock's physical bulk and powers up Body Press, turning Regirock into a bulky setup sweeper capable of suffocating physical attackers like Glastrier and overwhelming walls like Sandaconda. Thunder Wave slows down setup sweepers like Hisuian Sneasel and fast Pokemon like Whimsicott but makes Regirock more passive. Clear Body helpfully denies stat debuffs from Intimidate, Shadow Ball, and Triple Arrows. 16 Defense EVs and an Impish nature is a useful jump point to balance Regirock's bulk. Tera Poison helps with Regirock's Grass and Fighting weaknesses and gives it an immunity to poison.

Regirock fits best on teams looking for a physically defensive backbone that can set up Stealth Rock. While Regirock has great physical bulk, its typing and comparatively lesser special bulk leave it more prone to strong special attacks. Specially defensive partners like Muk and Articuno complement Regirock's physical bulk and can intercept special attackers like Clawitzer, Whimsicott, and Mesprit. Sandslash and Hitmontop can form entry hazard cores with Regirock and help keep it from taking entry hazard chip damage. Other Grass-type partners like Alolan Exeggutor, Dipplin, and Virizion are also appreciated by Regirock, as they can cover its Ground, Grass, and Water weaknesses by taking on Sandslash, opposing Dipplin, and Clawitzer, while Regirock can take on threatening Flying-types like Minior and Articuno.

Drifblim.comments:
Other Options

Tera Water is an alternative Tera type that lets Drifblim have an easier time against Basculin and Ice-types such as Beartic and Sneasel. However, it keeps the weakness to Electric-type moves.

Checks and Counters

Dark-types: Dark-types such as Brute Bonnet, Houndoom, Skuntank, and Sneasel can easily switch in on a non-boosted Hex and threaten it with their Dark-type STAB moves. However, aside from Houndoom, none of them appreciate switching in and being neutered by Will-O-Wisp.

Rock-types: Naclstack and Regirock easily check Drifblim with STAB Rock-type attacks. Naclstack, in particular, is immune to both Will-O-Wisp and Thunder Wave; it can force Drifblim out with Salt Cure or to Terastallize. Drifblim also must be wary of Lycanroc, which possesses STAB priority in Accelerock, often forcing Drifblim out. However, Lycanroc risks being crippled by Will-O-Wisp.

Item Removal: Drifblim is extremely reliant on Heavy-Duty Boots or Terastallization to avoid the super effective chip damage from Stealth Rock. It fears Knock Off from Mesprit, while Choice item Hisuian Sneasel and Whimsicott both run Switcheroo.

Electric-types: Jolteon, Magneton, and Vikavolt all pose a threat to Drifblim, pressuring it with their STAB Electric-type moves. Jolteon and Magneton in particular can also chip it down while pivoting with Volt Switch.

Defog.description:
Drifblim uses its typing, access to Strength Sap, and status moves to carve out a niche in ZU as the tier's best Defog user. Strength Sap provides Drifblim a source of healing while checking physical wallbreakers like Hitmontop, Hisuian Sneasel, and Toxicroak. Will-O-Wisp further neuters physically offensive foes such as Basculin and the aforementioned Hisuian Sneasel and Toxicroak, but Thunder Wave is an alternative option that can be used over Will-O-Wisp to punish setup sweepers like Mesprit and Farigiraf. Hex complements status being spread, but Air Slash is another STAB move that can be used to threaten the likes of Hitmontop, Venusaur, and Whimsicott. Drifblim can run two different EV spreads; it can run maximum Speed investment to speed tie with the other base 80 Speed Pokemon such as Mesprit and Venusaur. Drifblim can also run a physically defensive spread to better withstand hits from foes like Basculin and Hitmontop. Tera Fairy lets Drifblim blank Dragon Pulse from Clawitzer while flipping the matchup against Dark-types such as Brute Bonnet, Mabosstiff, and Sneasel.

Drifblim fits on bulky offense and balance teams that benefit from its typing and ability to remove entry hazards. Drifblim has somewhat lackluster bulk, so it appreciates teammates that can take hits better than it can, like Muk and Naclstack. Pivots such as Basculin, Jolteon, and Mesprit all enjoy Drifblim removing entry hazards, which prevents them from being worn down. Pokemon such as Hitmontop, Hisuian Sneasel, Naclstack, Regirock, and Toxicroak help check the Dark-types like Brute Bonnet, Houndoom, and Sneasel that Drifblim otherwise struggles against. In particular, Naclstack and Regirock's typing allows them to check Houndoom defensively. Drifblim appreciates partnering with Ground-types such as Whiscash and Sandslash, as they check Electric- and Rock-types like Magneton, Jolteon, and Lycanroc that Drifblim fails to deal with. Choice Band or Life Orb Sneasel and Choice Specs Typhlosion can independently form a good offensive core with Drifblim. Typhlosion pressures Ice-types such as Abomasnow and Beartic, while Sneasel greatly appreciates burn and paralysis support. They both value entry hazard removal from Drifblim and in return, Drifblim handles Ground-types such as Sandaconda and Sandslash by providing burn support and having a free opportunity to use Strength Sap.

Spiritomb.comments:
Other Options

Physically offensive and mixed variants of Spiritomb can be used due to its access to Sucker Punch and Poltergeist, but there are far better options for physically offensive Dark-types like Mabosstiff and Sneasel, which boast superior Attack and Speed stats. Spiritomb can opt to use Pain Split or Will-O-Wisp instead of Psyshock on the offensive Trick Room set in order to improve its survivability and potentially ease setup at a heavy cost to its coverage, losing out on key targets like Virizion and Poliwrath.

Checks and Counters

Dark-types: Offensive Dark-type foes like Mabosstiff and Sneasel resist Spiritomb's STAB Dark Pulse and retaliate with excellent damage themselves, though they must be wary of physically defensive variants that can debilitate them with Will-O-Wisp.

Fairy-types: As the tier's best Fairy-type Pokemon, Whimsicott can take on Spiritomb's boosted attacks and threaten heavy damage with Moonblast. Tera Fairy walls like Mesprit and Dipplin can resist Spiritomb's STAB Dark Pulse to stop its sweep or further their own setup.

Wallbreakers: Spiritomb's lackluster natural bulk can be easily preyed upon by wallbreakers like Mabosstiff, Jolteon, and Vikavolt regardless of what set it's running, though they must be careful when switching in.

Encore: While it is immune to Prankster Encore from the likes of Whimsicott and Sableye, Spiritomb must be wary of a potential Encore from other foes like Mesprit, Beartic, and non-Prankster variants of the previously mentioned Whimsicott.

Special Walls: Specially defensive walls like Cryogonal and Articuno can wipe away Spiritomb's Nasty Plot boosts with Haze; in addition, Articuno can also phaze Spiritomb with Roar.

Offensive Trick Room.description:
Spiritomb utilizes its defensive profile to switch into foes like Primeape and Hisuian Sneasel, setting up and devastating teams in a tier almost completely lacking Fairy-type Pokemon. Dark Pulse serves as an amazing neutral STAB attack for a vast majority of the tier, threatening OHKOs on metagame staples like Jolteon and Mesprit after a Nasty Plot boost; it is the preferred STAB over Shadow Ball to avoid letting Normal-type Pokemon like Pyroar and Farigiraf switch in for free. Psyshock compliments Dark Pulse excellently with its ability to hit Fighting-types like Poliwrath and Virizion while targeting opposing special walls like Cryogonal and Tera Fairy Naclstack on their comparatively worse Defense. Tera Dark further amplifies the power of Spiritomb's Dark Pulse to OHKO as many foes as possible once boosted, securing guaranteed OHKOs on foes like Charizard.

Spiritomb works well alongside wallbreakers like Virizion, Glastrier, and Hisuian Sneasel, which can wallbreak for each other and enable Spiritomb's sweep. Spiritomb struggles with strong physical attacks from the likes of Mabosstiff and Sneasel, so it appreciates physical walls like Regirock and Weezing that can reliably switch into them. Special attackers like Jolteon, Vikavolt, and Mesprit pair well with Spiritomb to break down walls for each other. Teammates with pivoting moves like Basculin, Jolteon, and Primeape can help bring Spiritomb in safely to help ease its setup. Entry hazard support from the likes of Regirock, Sandslash, and Mesprit is also greatly appreciated to help whittle foes into range for Spiritomb to sweep. Pokemon that can threaten Whimsicott like Muk and Magneton are appreciated to help cover for Spiritomb's lone weakness to Fairy.

Abomasnow.overview: With the combination of Aurora Veil, Snow Warning, and a strong offensive profile, Abomasnow is ZU's sole viable snow setter and a threatening offensive tank. The Defense boost from snow even helps Abomasnow tank attacks like Glastrier's Close Combat and Lycanroc's Stone Edge. Abomasnow's snow pairs well with Beartic, enabling the latter's Slush Rush for formidable late-game sweeps. Aurora Veil is also invaluable support for teammates like Jolteon and Charizard, allowing them to more easily wreak havoc against the opposition. Abomasnow itself can dish out powerful STAB attacks courtesy of its threatening offensive stats and coverage. Abomasnow, however, is hindered by its low Speed and awful defensive typing, leaving it vulnerable to special attackers like Charizard and Magneton without the help of Terastallization. Without Heavy-Duty Boots, Abomasnow is very vulnerable to getting worn down by entry hazards. Abomasnow faces competition from other offensive Grass-types like Venusaur and Virizion for their faster Speed and better defensive profiles, though Abomasnow stands out for its unique ability and offensive typing.
Abomasnow.comments:
Other Options

Focus Blast nails Orthworm and deals more damage to special walls and tanks like Glastrier, though its shaky accuracy makes it an inconsistent option. On more offensive teams, Eject Pack can make Abomasnow a one-time pivot with Leaf Storm, while Icy Rock can be run to give Beartic more turns of snow for sweeping. However, not running Heavy-Duty Boots exacerbates Abomasnow's vulnerability to residual damage. Physical sets can surprise special walls like Muk and Cryogonal, though they struggle by lacking a strong physical Ice-type STAB move and being less effective overall. Leech Seed can be used to discourage special walls like Cryogonal and Muk from staying in on Abomasnow, though it can't afford dropping Aurora Veil or coverage.

Checks and Counters

Fire-types: Even when Abomasnow has the Defense boost under snow, Fire-types like Charizard, Houndoom, and Typhlosion can melt it with their powerful STAB moves and switch into its Grass-type attacks.

Special Walls: Despite Abomasnow's powerful STAB moves, special tanks like Articuno and Cryogonal can stomach its attacks and reliably recover the damage off before chipping it away with their own STAB attacks.

Steel-types: Orthworm stands out as one of Abomasnow's most difficult matchups, resisting both of its STAB moves, being immune to Earth Power, and being able to retaliate by setting up entry hazards or Iron Defense. Other Steel-types like Magneton and Perrserker are similarly threatening but hate Earth Power.

Faster Pokemon: Abomasnow's awful defensive typing and merely fine bulk leave it vulnerable to numerous attackers faster than it. Revenge killers and wallbreakers like Primeape, Virizion, and Lycanroc are just a few examples that can pick it off with some chip damage, even if they dislike switching into it.

Aurora Veil.description:
Abomasnow is a chilling offensive tank courtesy of its offensive typing and the combination of Snow Warning and Blizzard. Giga Drain provides consistent recovery for Abomasnow and hits Water-types like Clawitzer and Poliwrath, though Leaf Storm can be used for immediate burst damage at the cost of lowering its offensive stats and forgoing healing. Earth Power is Abomasnow's strongest option against Steel-types like Magneton as well as Typhlosion. Heavy-Duty Boots covers Abomasnow's Stealth Rock vulnerability so it can effectively tank attacks and set up Aurora Veil. While other Pokemon are preferred for Terastallization, Tera Water can help in a pinch to shield Abomasnow from Fire-types like Charizard and Typhlosion.

Abomasnow tends to fit on offensive and balanced builds thanks to its offensive typing and the support provided by Aurora Veil. Offensive pivots like Mesprit and Jolteon appreciate it to better take damage from potential Choice Scarf users like opposing Mesprit and Hisuian Sneasel; Jolteon also benefits from having a more threatening coverage move under snow with Ice-type Weather Ball. Beartic particularly stands out as a partner, benefiting from the Defense boost under snow and having its sweeping capabilities enabled with Slush Rush. Other setup sweepers like Minor, Hisuian Sneasel, and Nasty Plot Mesprit benefit from the extra bulk under Aurora Veil giving them more opportunities to break or sweep. Abomasnow's plethora of weaknesses leaves it vulnerable to U-turn users and Fire-types; defensive backbones like Poliwrath, Mesprit, and Muk can provide support with their bulk and utility moves. Abomasnow can, in return, switch into weaker walls like Dipplin and Sandslash to threaten them away with powerful STAB moves. Hazard deterrents like Hattrem and Sandslash stand out as notable teammates, with the former providing Magic Bounce, paralysis, and Healing Wish support, while the latter can remove hazards and provide Knock Off utility.

Magmortar.comments:
Other Options

Magmortar can run a Choice Specs set consisting of Fire Blast, Thunderbolt, Scorching Sands, and Focus Blast, but in addition to the item locking it into one move, it would have to give up Heavy-Duty Boots, necessitating entry hazard-removing teammates.

Checks and Counters

Water-types: Water-types like Clawitzer and Basculin can easily threaten Magmortar with their STAB moves and force it to either switch out or Terastallize, although Magmortar is capable of hitting them with Thunderbolt as they switch in. Basculin in particular is able to outspeed Magmortar and easily pivot into another teammate while chipping it down.

Ground-types: Ground-type attackers such as Sandslash and Sandaconda threaten Magmortar with their STAB moves. Sandaconda is able to set up against it, but it must be wary of its own low Special Defense. Whiscash and Camerupt are also able to take on Magmortar with ease, setting up entry hazards while walling its attacks.

Rock-types: Magmortar struggles to break through specially defensive Regirock without Focus Blast, and Regirock can threaten it with STAB Rock-type moves. It must also be wary of Naclstack, which acts as a great wall against Magmortar due to its reliable recovery, Purifying Salt ability that shields it from burns, and access to Eviolite. Lycanroc's access to Rock-type STAB moves also threatens Magmortar, but it must be cautious of Scorching Sands on the switch in.

Special Walls: Muk is able to set up against Magmortar, and it doesn't mind the damage or burns thanks to Rest; it can take several hits from Magmortar and wear it down with Knock Off and Poison Jab. Eviolite Naclstack and Mesprit also take Magmortar's hits very well, and the former can threaten it with Salt Cure.

Fast Revenge Killers: Toxicroak is able to outspeed Magmortar and OHKO it with Close Combat. Choice Specs Jolteon is also good at picking Magmortar off cleanly with Thunderbolt, as are Sneasel and Basculin with their STAB moves.

All-out Attacker.description:
Magmortar finds a place in the tier as a dangerous special attacker with a plethora of good coverage moves. Scorching Sands lets Magmortar hit other Fire-types such as Typhlosion and Camerupt as well as Rock-types like Regirock and Lycanroc. Thunderbolt damages Flying-types like Charizard and Drifblim and Water-types like Clawitzer, while Focus Blast can be run to hit the aforementioned Rock-types even harder, notably 2HKOing Regirock and Naclstack. Knock Off removes Heavy-Duty Boots from Hitmontop, Eviolite from Naclstack, and Leftovers from Regirock. Taunt can be used instead to stop status moves like Recover from Naclstack while blocking the use of Stealth Rock and Protect; this also prevents the use of Sticky Web from Vikavolt and Toxic Spikes from Muk. Heavy-Duty Boots is required for this set due to how much Magmortar despises entry hazards. Tera Ground powers up Scorching Sands, which notably lets Magmortar 3HKO specially defensive Muk and 2HKO Regirock, and also gives the Pokemon an Electric immunity that blocks Volt Switch from Jolteon. Tera Grass alternatively lets it resist Ground-type and Water-type attacks from Sandaconda, Sandslash, and Clawitzer.

Magmortar is best slotted onto balance teams that are looking for a powerful wallbreaker. It benefits from pivots that are able to bring it in safely, such as Magneton, Jolteon, and Basculin. Magmortar also appreciates teammates that check Water-types like Clawitzer and Poliwrath. Specially offensive teammates like Arboliva and Jolteon also benefit from Magmortar's ability to force significant damage onto Orthworm. Grass-types like Venusaur, Whimsicott, and Arboliva make great partners for Magmortar, as they cover its weaknesses and can break some of its checks in Clawitzer, Basculin, and Sandslash. Magmortar serves as a good switch-in to Will-O-Wisp from Drifblim and Weezing directed at Hitmontop, which can in turn take care of Regirock. Faster revenge killers such as Sneasel are also taken care of by Regirock and Sableye, which both appreciate Magmortar's ability to deal with other common physical attackers like Glastrier, Brute Bonnet, and Perrserker.

Mesprit.overview: Mesprit is a highly flexible Pokemon that is capable of fulfulling a multitude of roles within the ZU metagame. Its balanced stats and useful movepool allow Mesprit to work well in offensive and defensive utility roles, while its Nasty Plot set is a threatening sweeper that is extremely challenging for bulky builds to deal with. Mesprit is also a good check to Fighting-types such as Virizion and Primeape, and Levitate lets it deal with many Ground-types, such as Sandslash and Sandaconda. Unfortunately, Mesprit's balanced stats means that it isn't truly exceptional at any role that it tries to play, as its offensive stats aren't fantastic, and bulky sets are prone to getting chipped down over time. Its Psychic typing also leaves it vulnerable to common Dark-types, such as Sneasel, Spiritomb, and Skuntank, while also leaving it weak to the ubiquitous U-turn. Finally, Mesprit's coverage is quite limited, as it lacks a way to hit Steel-types hard and doesn't hit much of the metagame for super effective damage. This can lead to it sometimes struggling to break through bulky threats such as Vikavolt and Glastrier.
Mesprit.comments:
Other Options

A Choice Specs set utilizing Psychic, Dazzling Gleam, Psyshock or Shadow Ball, and either Trick or U-turn is feasible. However, Mesprit isn't powerful enough to consistently break down defensive cores without perfect prediction, it can't hit Steel-types for significant damage, and its mediocre Speed tier means it isn't especially good against offensive teams either. The Nasty Plot set can consider Psychic Noise or Stored Power as its Psychic-type STAB move. The former prevents recovery from the likes of Articuno, Naclstack, and Sandaconda, while the latter will become extremely powerful if Mesprit accrues multiple Nasty Plot boosts. However, Psyshock's ability to pressure common special walls like Naclstack and Articuno makes it the most consistent option. The defensive pivot set can consider Colbur Berry as an alternative item to take Knock Off from the likes of Primeape and Sneasel, as well as Dark-type attacks from Spiritomb. However, the loss of Leftovers recovery means that only offensive teams looking to play fast should consider this, and it loses value if Mesprit is forced to Terastallize. The defensive pivot could also use other Tera types, such as Tera Fairy to give a Dark resistance that is useful when facing down Sneasel, Spiritomb, and Primeape and Tera Water to grant useful resistances for Typhlosion and Clawitzer. Tera Water, however, leaves Mesprit open to common Grass- and Electric-type attacks from Jolteon and Virizion. Options like Thunder Wave to deter setup or and Pain Split to give Mesprit some form of recovery may seem appealing for the defensive set, but Mesprit tends to perform its role more consistently with the given moves.

Checks and Counters

Dark-types and Dark-type Coverage: Dark-types such as Sneasel, Spiritomb, and Persian-Alola threaten Mesprit with their STAB moves and either force it to switch out or Terastallize. Mesprit is capable of pivoting out or hitting them with Fairy-type coverage as they switch in, however. Pokemon that Mesprit would otherwise check, such as Primeape, Hisuian Sneasel, and Sandslash often run Dark-type coverage, limiting its ability to comfortably deal with them. Sableye and Spiritomb don't fear Mesprit's STAB moves, and Sableye can threaten to lock Mesprit into an ineffective move with Prankster Encore, though neither of them appreciate Fairy-type coverage, Knock Off, or Trick.

Ghost-types: Ghost-type attackers such as Froslass and Rotom threaten Mesprit with their STAB moves, but they can't comfortably switch in to most sets due to the threat of Knock Off, Trick, or a STAB move.

Steel-types: Steel-types such as Orthworm, Magneton, and Perrserker resist Mesprit's common coverage options, and they can use it as an opportunity to fire off a strong attack or, in Orthworm's case, set up Stealth Rock, Spikes, Coil, or Iron Defense. However, they must be wary of Trick or Knock Off. Additionally, certain Pokemon Mesprit usually checks, such as Weezing and Articuno, often run Tera Steel and can therefore turn the tables on Mesprit.

Strong Wallbreakers: Mesprit's rather modest bulk means it can't comfortably answer many of the tier's strongest offensive threats. Typhlosion and Jolteon outrun non-Choice Scarf Mesprit and threaten to do serious damage, even to the defensive set. Even slow wallbreakers such as Alolan Exeguttor and Clawitzer are still faster than the defensive pivot set and threaten it with extreme damage.

Physically Defensive Pivot.description:
Mesprit acts as a great defensive pivot thanks to its solid physical bulk with investment, as it can check physical attackers like Hisuian Sneasel and Primeape. On top of Mesprit's bulk and defensive profile enabling it to act as a reliable Stealth Rock setter, it also has good matchups against common entry hazard removers like Sandslash and Hitmontop. However, Mesprit can drop Stealth Rock to fit Healing Wish, a rare and especially good support move that helps more offensive teams. Psychic Noise is Mesprit's preferred STAB move for its secondary effect of stifling recovery from walls like Naclstack and Glastrier. U-turn is crucial for Mesprit to keep up momentum, especially against Dark-type switch-ins like Alolan Persian, Skuntank, and Sneasel. Knock Off rounds out Mesprit's moveset, compressing even more utility and punishing Dark-weak or item-reliant Pokemon like opposing Mesprit and Magneton. However, Mesprit has a handful of other utility options that it can fit over Knock Off, including Encore, which punishes setup sweepers like Shell Smash Minior, Nasty Plot Mesprit, and Iron Defense Regirock looking to break through or take advantage of Mesprit. If Mesprit can't afford to fit Healing Wish over Stealth Rock, then it can use Healing Wish in its last slot to compress hazard and cleric support in one slot. As Mesprit tends to not need to stick around as a defensive pivot, it often runs Rocky Helmet to push as much passive damage onto the opposing team as possible, especially against spinners like Sandslash and pivots like Basculin. 96 Speed EVs help Mesprit outspeed neutral-natured base 60 Speed Pokemon like Abomasnow as well as slower threats like Clawitzer. Mesprit can use Tera Steel for fantastic defensive synergy with Levitate on top of a great defensive typing in general to better answer Whimsicott and Dipplin and surprise physical attackers like Glastrier and Lycanroc. Tera Steel has its own drawbacks, including giving Mesprit a weakness to Fighting, which worsens its matchups against Fighting-types and users of Fighting-type coverage moves, such as Virizion and Glastrier, respectively. Alternatively, Mesprit can consider Tera Fairy as its Tera typing to gain useful resistances to Dark and Bug to take Knock Off and U-turn from Primeape and Poliwrath better, and this can give Mesprit critical breathing room against Sneasel to use U-turn. However, Mesprit's role as a support Pokemon means it often does not want to exhaust a resource as powerful as Terastallization unless it absolutely has to.

As a physically defensive pivot with plenty of utility options and role compression, Mesprit is capable of fitting on a variety of teams and adjusting its set for its team's needs. Bulky offense teams and balance teams alike enjoy Mesprit's capabilities of acting as a midground against physical attackers and bringing in its more offensive teammates with U-turn. Wallbreakers like Hisuian Sneasel, Clawitzer, and Choice Specs Whimsicott enjoy the free entry provided by Mesprit, and the last two create pivot cores with Mesprit. Other pivots like Basculin, Jolteon, and Primape make great cores with Mesprit for their combined ability to keep up momentum. Sandslash is a standout partner with Mesprit, as the two can compress plenty of utility moves between the two of them; Mesprit can run Stealth Rock to free Sandslash up to run coverage moves or Spikes, while Sandslash has Knock Off to free up Mesprit to run Encore or Healing Wish. Healing Wish variants are appreciated by Pokemon that lack recovery or tend to rely on Rest, including Regirock, Sandslash, and Perrserker. Basculin often wears itself down with recoil damage from Wave Crash on top of repeatedly taking damage from entry hazards as an offensive pivot, and Mesprit can relieve it from KOing itself thanks to Healing Wish. Healing Wish is also much appreciated by setup sweepers, as it gives them a free switch in and often allows bulkier sweepers like Spiritomb a second chance at winning. Mesprit can't do much against Steel-types like Perrserker and Orthworm on its own, so it depends on Fighting-type teammates like Hisuian Sneasel, Primeape, and Virizion in its stead. Conveniently, these Fighting-types also match up well against Dark-types, such as Alolan Persian and Sneasel, that would threaten Mesprit. Teammates like Whimsicott, Sneasel, and Houndoom can help manage Mesprit's problematic matchups against Spiritomb and Sableye.

Choice Scarf.description:
Choice Scarf Mesprit is a great revenge killer to handle fast Pokemon like Jolteon and Hisuian Sneasel. Dazzling Gleam is Mesprit's best complementary coverage move for Psychic, covering Dark-types like Sneasel and Sableye. U-turn lets Mesprit keep up offensive momentum against switch-ins like Orthworm, Farigiraf, and Skuntank. Healing Wish lets Mesprit give teammates like Virizion, Jolteon, and Glastrier a second chance to set up and sweep. Mesprit can alternatively use Trick to make a defensive Pokemon like Cryogonal or Orthworm less threatening. Tera Fairy Mesprit is a more effective offensive and defensive answer to Sneasel and Primeape given their Dark-type coverage moves, and Tera Fairy lets Mesprit not play guessing games with Psychic and Dazzling Gleam against Tera Dark Primeape. Tera Steel has good synergy with Levitate and gives Mesprit several new resistances to more easily answer Whimsicott, Sandslash, and Farigiraf.

Offensive pivots like Jolteon and Magneton make great cores with Mesprit and appreciate Mesprit's Levitate to more easily play around Ground-types. Fighting-types like Virizion, Primeape, and Hitmontop are great offensive checks to Steel-types like Orthworm and Perrserker, which give Mesprit trouble, and they appreciate Mesprit offensively checking Poison-types like Weezing and Hisuian Sneasel. Mesprit benefits from Ground-type teammates like Sandslash and Sandaconda patching up its poor matchup against Sneasel-Hisui, while it can check foes like Virizion for them. Setup sweepers like Primeape, Regirock, and Virizion benefit from Mesprit providing them second chances to sweep with Healing Wish or making foes more exploitable with Trick. Physical wallbreakers like Glastrier and Hisuian Sneasel all benefit from Healing Wish giving them an additional chance for breaking, as they can break special walls that Mesprit cannot.

Nasty Plot Sweeper.description:
Nasty Plot Mesprit is a fearsome mid- or late-game sweeper thanks to the combination of Levitate and a somewhat high base 105 Special Attack stat. Its biggest perk is the ability to set up in front of most bulkier Pokémon. Mesprit can choose between Encore and Substitute as its setup tool; Substitute can be very good versus bulkier teams, as Mesprit can tank a hit from most of the passive mons such as Regirock and Orthworm if not being hit super effectively, obtaining a free turn to either set up Nasty Plot or fire off an attack. Encore helps to circumvent Mesprit's passivity and can take advantage of checks that need to set up or use status moves like Haze to wall Mesprit, like Articuno for the Haze case, and Regirock for the setup. Psyshock is great to deal with specially defensive Pokémon such as Naclstack. Draining Kiss allows Mesprit to outlast Dark-type checks such as Spiritomb and Sneasel while healing in the process, Thunderbolt allows it to hit and Flying- and Water-types such as Charizard and Clawitzer hard. Tera Fairy lets Mesprit set up in front of the aforementioned Dark-types, grants immunity to Dragon Tail from Dipplin and Appletun, and increases the power of Draining Kiss, letting Mesprit restore more HP on the aforementioned threats and making it even harder to take down. Tera Steel improves Mesprit's defensive capabilities, making it take neutral damage from Dark- and Ghost-type attacks and giving it a Bug resistance, while only being weak to Fighting- and Fire-type moves due to Levitate. Tera Electric boosts the power of Thunderbolt and removes all of Mesprit's weaknesses.

Nasty Plot Sweeper Mesprit works well with specific entry hazard setters and Fighting-types. Hazard setters like Orthworm and Naclstack both cover each other's weaknesses and let Mesprit get rolls it wouldn't otherwise, such as KOing Honchkrow with Tera Fairy Draining Kiss after Stealth Rock chip damage. Fighting-types such as Virizion and Primeape deserve a highlight, as when paired with Mesprit, they provide good coverage for it, pressuring Dark- and Steel-types with their STAB moves and Ghost-types with Knock Off. Both of them offer additional utility, with Virizion being bulky enough to enter in and provide wallbreaking prowess and Primeape having access to U-turn to let Mesprit in safely and Knock Off to offer utility. Secondary wallbreakers that don't rely on Setup to make progress such as Clawitzer and Magneton will also appreciate being by Mesprit's side. Some cleaners also enjoy Mesprit's ability to make progress so they can clean late-game, such as Dodrio and Hisuian Sneasel.

Specially Defensive Pivot.description:
Mesprit's decent defensive profile and access to useful utility moves like Stealth Rock and Healing Wish allow it to act as a good defensive pivot. Its bulk and typing allows it to check foes such as Jolteon, Charizard, and Primeape locked into Close Combat. Psychic Noise is a useful STAB move that allows Mesprit to force out Hitmonchan and Hitmontop, which are among the few viable hazard removers of the tier. Its decent Base Power and ability to prevent healing allow Mesprit to annoy other defensive Pokemon like Dipplin and Sandaconda. U-turn allows Mesprit to pivot out of poor matchups such as Glastrier and Spiritomb while keeping up momentum for its team. Knock Off allows Mesprit to punish foes that would feel comfortable coming in otherwise, such as Orthworm, Articuno, and Skuntank. Healing Wish is another option for offensive teams that allows Mesprit to fully heal an offensive teammate and give it another go at breaking down the opponent's team. It is capable of running both Knock Off and Healing Wish if your team already has another Stealth Rock user. Maximum Special Defense investment is preferred in order to better take hits from the likes of Charizard, Magneton, and Pyroar. A Sassy nature is preferred to give Mesprit a slow U-turn and maximize the damage that its physical attacks do. Steel is the preferred Tera type, as it turns each of Mesprit's weaknesses into a neutrality or a resistance and gives it a superb general defensive typing. It notably lets Mesprit handle opposing Mesprit and Whimsicott with more confidence. Tera Steel also has good synergy with Levitate and allows Mesprit to comfortably handle foes such as Sandslash and Sandaconda. Leftovers grants Mesprit passive recovery and helps it stay as healthy as possible throughout the match.

Mesprit is one of the few bulky Stealth Rock setters in the tier that also has access to a pivoting move, which makes it highly useful for both balance and offense. Mesprit lacks reliable recovery, so teams utilizing it shouldn't expect it to be a stalwart wall. Rather, its good support movepool and defensive traits allow it to act as a stopgap that can take a few hits while also supporting its offensively oriented teammates via pivoting and utility moves such as Knock Off and Healing Wish. Mesprit's physical bulk is somewhat lacking, so physically defensive teammates such as Weezing and Sandaconda, make for good partners. These two help out against Fighting-types such as Primeape and Hisuian Sneasel, which Mesprit struggles to check in the long term. Dipplin serves as a good physically defensive partner and in particular a Knock Off absorber, which is highly useful for Mesprit because it fears Knock Off greatly. Mesprit is also vulnerable to Dark-types such as Spiritomb and Sneasel. Teammates such as Hisuian Sneasel and Virizion can help with the latter, while Articuno and Whimsicott can help with the former. Teammates such as Primeape, Virizion, and Charizard can help check Steel-types such as Magneton and Orthworm, which Mesprit struggles to deal with on its own. Strong wallbreakers, such as Typhlosion, Glastrier, and Alolan Exeggutor, appreciate Mesprit's slow U-turn getting them in safely, and they benefit from Mesprit's ability to use Knock Off to remove various items from defensive Pokemon such as Regirock, Orthworm, and Articuno. Mesprit also pairs well with other VoltTurn users; Primeape and Whimsicott also benefit from Mesprit bringing them in safely and can form a pivoting core that can slowly wear down the opponent's team. Pokemon such as Typhlosion, Charizard, and Glastrier appreciate Healing Wish support should Mesprit choose to run it, as they are all vulnerable to being worn down over the course of a match, and many teams are not prepared to deal with them a second time.

Whimsicott.overview: Blessed with the rare Fairy typing and an excellent Speed tier, Whimsicott makes for an excellent revenge killer and utility Pokemon. Whimsicott's unique typing allows it to beat many dangerous threats like Persian-Alola and Virizion. A powerful Moonblast and pivoting coupled with Choice Specs allow Whimsicott to break past bulkier threats like Orthworm and Glastrier. However, Whimsicott's low offensive stats leave it often wanting for power, especially without Choice Specs, and it's incapable of fitting every move it wants. Furthermore, lack of reliable recovery and only average bulk makes it unable to consistently check the Pokemon it's supposed to like Regirock and Sandaconda.
Whimsicott.comments:
Other Options

Psychic is an option that nails Poison-types like Weezing. However, this comes at the cost of either losing U-turn's pivoting or the utility of Switcheroo or Encore. Whimsicott can use a SubSeed set to mess up its usual defensive checks like Cryogonal and Orthworm and be a general nuisance. This set struggles to find setup opportunities however and relies heavily on getting a free Substitute on a forced switch.

Checks and Counters

Poison-types: Whimsicott's 4x weakness to Poison and inability to hit Poison-types with its STAB attacks makes Poison-types like Muk and Weezing good checks. However, these Pokemon lack reliable recovery and can thus be worn down over a game.

Steel-types: Steel-types like Magneton and Orthworm can take Whimsicott's STAB attacks with ease. Orthworm can start setting entry hazards or set up with Coil or Iron Defense, while Magneton can generate momentum with Volt Switch.

Fire-types: As Whimsicott's STAB attacks do little to them, Fire-types like Charizard, Typhlosion, and Magmortar can use Whimsicott as an entry point to start firing off powerful attacks. These Pokemon aren't usually defensively invested however and can struggle to switch in consistently.

Special Walls: Special walls like Cryogonal and Articuno aren't bothered by Whimsicott's attacks and can use it to start forcing progress with entry hazards, status, pivoting, or boosts. However, Switcheroo completely ruins these walls, especially Articuno, and Encore can force them to repeat their status moves, allowing Whimsicott to safely bring in a teammate.

Choice Scarf Users: Many common Choice Scarf users like Hisuian Sneasel and Gunk Shot Primeape get the jump on Whimsicott and can easily take it out.

Choice Specs.description:
Choice Specs Whimsicott takes advantage of its high Speed and Moonblast to be a powerful cleaner and revenge killer. U-turn lets It scout for switches and pivot out of unfavorable matchups like Weezing and Orthworm, allowing it to bring in a more suitable teammate. Switcheroo allows Whimsicott to cripple its defensive checks, greatly hindering special walls like Orthworm and especially Articuno, letting Whimsicott and its teammates break past them later in the game. However, Whimsicott loses a substantial amount of power without its Choice Specs, so one must be careful about when to use Switcheroo. Infiltrator allows Whimsicott to bypass Substitute from foes like Glastrier and opposing Whimsicott.

Choice Specs Whimsicott best fits on balance and bulky offense teams looking for a fast offensive cleaner that also provides some defensive utility. Whimsicott loves entry-hazard stacking teammates like Sandslash and Orthworm to help push its otherwise average damage output. It can't handle Fire-types like Charizard and Typhlosion, so answers to them like Regirock and Naclstack help immensely. Whimsicott can in return take advantage of the Grass- and Fighting-types like Virizion and Primeape that these Pokemon are at a disadvantage against. Whimsicott struggles with Poison-types like Weezing and Muk who resist its STAB attacks and threathen it with 4x super effective attacks, so answers like Magneton and Sandaconda make for great teammates. Whimsicott pairs well with offensive Fire-types like Typhlosion and Charizard that handle the Steel-types like Orthworm that it has difficulties breaking past, and in return, it handles their Water and Rock-type answers such as Clawitzer and Regirock.

Offensive Pivot.description:
Whimsicott leverages its superb Speed and access to Prankster to be a competent offensive pivot. Giga Drain is used for its ability to give Whimsicott staying power in longer games. Encore is key to this set, allowing it to force defensive foes into unfavorable moves like Stealth Rock and Recover, as well as shutting down setup sweepers like Jolteon and Regirock by locking them into their setup moves. U-turn pairs beautifully with Encore, scouting any forced switches and allowing Whimsicott to bring in powerful teammates like Glastrier and Typhlosion. Tera Steel gives Whimsicott an immunity to Poison-type attacks from Muk and Weezing, and a resistance to Flying-type attacks from Dodrio as well as Ice-type attacks from Sneasel in a pinch. However, this still leaves Whimsicott vulnerable to Fire-type attacks from Charizard and Typhlosion and makes it weak to Fighting, making it unable to reliably switch into Primeape and Virizion. Tera Fire provides emergency resistances to Ice and Fire, but this makes Whimsicott unable to safely check Ground- and Rock-types like Sandaconda and Regirock.

Balance teams enjoy Whimsicott's pivoting and Prankster Encore helping them deal with bulky defensive Pokemon and dangerous setup sweepers. Powerful physical wallbreakers like Glastrier and Primeape pair well with Whimsicott, as it baits in special walls like Articuno and can bring these teammates in with U-turn. Ground-types like Sandaconda make for excellent partners, as Whimsicott helps them handle the Water- and Grass-types like Clawitzer and Virizion that they struggle with, while they can handle Poison-types like Muk that scare Whimsicott out. Fire-types like Charizard and Magmortar can easily gain entry on Whimsicott, so Fire-resistant teammates like Regirock and Naclstack are helpful partners. Partners such as Charizard or Primeape that threaten Steel-types like Orthworm and Magneton can come in after a U-turn on these Steel-types to fire off attacks. Electric-types like Jolteon and Magneton appreciate Whimsicott's ability to handle Ground-types like Sandaconda, and in return, they handle the Flying-types like Dodrio and Articuno that Whimsicott struggles to beat. Other offensive pivots like Primeape and Mesprit can form a powerful VoltTurn core with Whimsicott, quickly racking up damage against walls like Regirock and Muk, especially with entry hazards up.

Beartic.overview: Under snow, Beartic is a chillingly threatening sweeper, boasting wide coverage and frightening offensive power while outspeeding the entire unboosted metagame. The Defense boost in snow allows it to tank physical attacks like Primeape's Close Combat and Lycanroc's Stone Edge. Beartic relies on Abomasnow to provide snow, and it forms a deadly offensive core with it, as Abomasnow's Aurora Veil makes Beartic even more difficult to take down. Beartic, however, is hindered by its low Speed and awful defensive typing; even under snow, it can be outsped by faster Choice Scarf users like Hisuian Sneasel and Typhlosion. Even with the Defense boost and Aurora Veil support, Beartic sports minimal defensive utility, owing to its vulnerability to all residual damage and lack of resistances if not running Heavy-Duty Boots, and it also stacks weaknesses with Abomasnow. Outside of snow, Beartic is extremely slow and easy to revenge kill without Terastallization, requiring significant support to perform well as a setup sweeper.
Beartic.comments:
Other Options

Beartic has no other viable options.

Checks and Counters

Faster Revenge Killers: Even under snow, Beartic is still quite slow and can be picked off by Choice Scarf users like Basculin, Hisuian Sneasel, and Primeape.

Physical Walls: Physical walls like Weezing, Spiritomb, and Sableye can threaten Beartic with Will-O-Wisp, while walls like Regirock and Orthworm can beat it with Iron Defense in a one-on-one scenario.

Residual Damage: Beartic is vulnerable to all entry hazards if not running Heavy-Duty Boots and despises status, all of which heavily cut into its sweeping potential and making it easier to revenge kill.

Wallbreakers: Most wallbreakers not weak to its coverage moves can stomach at least one unboosted attack and retaliate, which is especially prominent if Beartic is not under snow. Examples include Primeape, Vikavolt, and Clawitzer.

Glastrier: Glastrier can leverage its massive bulk under snow and retaliate with its own coverage moves to defeat Beartic.

Snow Sweeper.description:
The combination of snow and Slush Rush enables Beartic to become one of the scariest setup sweepers under this archetype, boasting high offensive stats and outspeeding the entire unboosted metagame. Icicle Spear's higher power is preferred over Icicle Crash's potential flinch chance if running Loaded Dice, and its multiple hits work well against foes using Substitute like Minior and Whimsicott. Close Combat threatens Ice-resistant targets like Orthworm and Glastrier while also hitting Regirock hard. Earthquake threatens Magneton, and non-Choice Scarf Typhlosion, while Substitute capitalizes on weak walls like Muk and Dipplin. While an Adamant nature is ideal to maximize its power, Jolly can be used to outspeed Choice Scarf Primeape and Choice Scarf Mesprit under snow. If Beartic is not running Icicle Spear, Life Orb or Heavy-Duty Boots can be chosen to either strengthen all of its moves' power or protect it from entry hazard damage, respectively. Tera Fighting boosts the power of Close Combat and lets Beartic resist priority moves like Sucker Punch and Accelerock, while Tera Ground boosts the power of Earthquake and provides it a Stealth Rock resistance and Electric immunity while still resisting Accelerock; changing into either of these Tera types, however, relinquishes the Defense boost under snow.

Due to Beartic being heavily reliant on snow for wallbreaking, Abomasnow is a mandatory partner to enable Slush Rush and the Defense boost. Pivots like Clawitzer and Primeape can help threaten and force out walls like Regirock and Orthworm, while wallbreakers like Clawitzer and Charizard can be similarly effective. Hattrem is a notable partner, as it supplies paralysis and Healing Wish support, enabling Beartic to clean through teams easier, while providing Magic Bounce support for Beartic's non-Heavy-Duty Boots sets. Spinners like Sandslash and Hitmontop are also valuable partners, providing entry hazard removal support without needing to remove Aurora Veil. Water-types like Clawitzer and Basculin can not only force out Fire-types but also provide pivoting support. Hazard setters like Regirock and Sandslash not only can help rack up residual damage over the course of a game but also provide additional utility with Thunder Wave and Knock Off, respectively. Other setup sweepers like Minior can overwhelm teams and shared checks like Regirock with Beartic.

Virizion.overview: Virizion's great Speed tier and threatening offensive typing make it a dangerous offensive threat that can serve as both a wallbreaker and a late-game cleaner. Virizion has access to Swords Dance, which allows it to boost its attack and wallbreak consistently. Its great Speed tier lets it stay threatening versus offensive teams and even clean them up late-game when its checks are weakened or removed. Virizion has naturally high special bulk and a workable defensive typing that allow it to find set up opportunities versus Choice-locked special attackers like Jolteon, Magneton, and Clawitzer, and there is some ambiguity to its sets thanks to its good coverage and ability to run a physical or special set. However, Virizion isn't especially strong without a boost, and it can struggle to break through physically bulky checks like Weezing and Dipplin. Despite that, Virizion retains a very solid niche thanks to its higher Speed tier and its ability to run coverage options like Stone Edge and Zen Headbutt.
Virizion.comments:
Other Options

Virizion can run a Tera Flying Calm Mind set using Giga Drain, Air Slash, and Focus Blast or Aura Sphere. This turns the tables on many of Virizion's traditional answers such as Dipplin and Weezing. However, Virizion can only boost its Special Attack by one stage at a time with Calm Mind, and it is forced to use weak moves unless it opts for the inaccurate Focus Blast as its Fighting-type STAB move, which makes the set considerably less threatening in practice. A mixed attacker set with Leaf Storm, Close Combat, Stone Edge, and Synthesis with a Life Orb can be threatening to certain offensive and balance teams but fails to make significant progress versus builds packing common walls such as Dipplin, Mesprit, and Muk. Sacred Sword is an interesting alternative to Close Combat that ignores the Defense boosts on IronPress foes such as Regirock, Orthworm, and Naclstack and doesn't lower Virizion's own defenses, but the drop in power is very noticeable. Tera Fairy is a fringe alternative Tera type that gives Virizion a Fighting resistance, allowing it to stave off revenge killing from Choice Scarf Primeape, and an immunity to Dragon Tail, which lets it potentially break past Dipplin after accruing multiple Swords Dance boosts. Tera Poison grants Virizion a generally useful defensive typing to counteract revenge killing attempts from the likes of Whimsicott and Primeape. These are inferior to the other Tera types outside of these specific situations, however.

Checks and Counters

Dipplin: Dipplin's massive physical bulk allows it to handily deal with the standard Swords Dance set. It isn't even 2HKOed by a +2 Close Combat and can use Dragon Tail to phaze Virizion before healing off any damage, which makes it a very reliable answer. It has to be wary of a surprise Calm Mind set, however, and the rare Tera Fairy can turn it into setup fodder.

Poison-types: Bulky Poison-types such as Weezing and Muk resist Virizion's STAB moves and threaten it in return with their STAB moves, or in Weezing's case, a burn from Will-O-Wisp. They must be cautious of Zen Headbutt, however. Hisuian Sneasel is faster than Virizion and can OHKO it with Gunk Shot. Additionally, several Pokemon that Virizion usually threatens, such as Sandslash and Regirock, commonly run Tera Poison and can therefore turn the tables on it.

Revenge Killers: Virizion's Speed tier, while very solid, still leaves it outpaced by several common revenge killers. Choice Scarf Primeape, Whimsicott, Sneasel, and Choice Scarf Typhlosion all outrun Virizion and can threaten to revenge kill it. All but the latter two need chip damage dealt before they can KO Virizion, however, and only Whimsicott and Primeape can directly switch in.

Status: Burns and paralysis are especially threatening to Virizion. Common Pokemon such as Weezing, Mesprit and Charizard are all capable of spreading those status conditions, and Virizion is not capable of OHKOing any of them except for Charizard if it chooses to run Stone Edge. However, Lum Berry allows Virizion to circumvent this and set up on many of these foes fearlessly.

Swords Dance.description:
Swords Dance allows Virizion to break through bulky foes like Mesprit and Regirock. Stone Edge lets Virizion threaten Charizard and Articuno. Zen Headbutt can be used instead to threaten Poison-types like Weezing and Muk. Justified dissuades foes such as Primeape and Poliwrath from attempting to remove Virizion's item via Knock Off. Heavy-Duty Boots allows Virizion to come in multiple times throughout a game without fear of chip damage from entry hazards. However, Lum Berry is a viable alternative on offensive teams, as it allows Virizion to set up safely on foes that might try to cripple it with status, such as Mesprit, Rotom, and Sandaconda. Tera Rock allows Stone Edge to OHKO Whimsicott and 2HKO Weezing after Stealth Rock damage at +2. It also gives Virizion a resistance to Fire- and Flying-type attacks, which lets it deal with Charizard and Typhlosion with more confidence. Tera Psychic lets Zen Headbutt 2HKO Tera Poison Regirock at +2, do significantly more damage to Weezing, removes Virizion's weakness to their Poison-type STAB moves, and gives Virizion a useful Fighting resistance that can foil revenge killing attempts from Choice Scarf Primeape and opposing Virizion.

Virizion's great Speed tier and ability to both wallbreak and sweep depending on the situation make it usable on teams ranging from balanced to hyper offensive team styles. Virizion dislikes facing down extremely bulky physical walls such as Dipplin and Weezing and Iron Defense Pokemon such as Regirock and Orthworm, which can also outlast Virizion should they begin boosting as it comes in. Strong special attackers, such as Nasty Plot Mesprit, Magneton, and Charizard, are therefore good teammates; the former is especially good at disrupting these extremely bulky threats. These teammates appreciate Virizion's Speed tier helping them out against offensive builds and its ability to threaten special walls like Cryogonal. Virizion appreciates entry hazards on the opponent's side, as it tends to force a lot of switches and loves seeing its defensive checks worn down. Stealth Rock setters such as Mesprit and Regirock are good teammates as a result, as are Spikes setters like Orthworm. These bulky hazard setters help to check revenge killers that are dangerous to Virizion; Regirock and Naclstack can help check Choice Scarf Typhlosion; Mesprit helps out versus Choice Scarf Primeape. Virizion pairs well with other physical wallbreakers, especially on offense and hyper offense, as they can work together to break down their shared defensive answers. Teammates such as Swords Dance Sneasel and Glastrier work well as a result.

Clawitzer.comments:
Other Options

Flash Cannon is Clawitzer's strongest option against Articuno and also hits Whimsicott super effectively, but it has poor coverage and power otherwise. Hydro Pump's high Base Power seems tempting, but its low accuracy makes it unreliable compared to Water Pulse.

Checks and Counters

Grass-types: ZU's many Grass-types pose a threat to Clawitzer, as they can easily switch into its Water Pulse and threaten an OHKO. Venusaur and Virizion can switch into Clawitzer multiple times thanks to their higher Speed and recovery in Synthesis. They must be cautious of Dragon Pulse, however. Brute Bonnet, Arboliva, and Whimsicott threaten Clawitzer as well but have to be wary of its coverage moves.

Electric-types: Jolteon, Vikavolt, and Magneton all threaten Clawitzer while also pivoting on it with Volt Switch. However, they struggle to switch directly into Clawitzer due to its powerful attacks.

Faster Attackers: Clawitzer's low Speed means that most offensive Pokemon, such as Toxicroak, Hitmontop, and Skunktank, are able to outrun and KO it. However, these offensive foes are rarely able to switch in directly, and Clawitzer's decent bulk allows it to take a hit from them at high HP.

Wallbreaker.description:
With a high Special Attack and Mega Launcher, Clawitzer makes for a devastating wallbreaker in ZU. Water Pulse has as much power as Surf when boosted by Mega Launcher, with the added benefit of potentially confusing the foe. Dragon Pulse serves as a powerful neutral attack that hits opposing Clawitzer and Venusaur while also destroying Alolan Exeggutor. Aura Sphere hits Glastrier, Brute Bonnet, and Arboliva hard, which would otherwise be able to switch into Clawitzer's attacks. Clawitzer's last moveslot is rather flexible; Dark Pulse hits Psychic- and Ghost-types like Mesprit and Froslass super effectively, and Sludge Bomb hits most Grass-types super effectively, notably OHKOing Whimsicott. U-turn forfeits coverage in exchange for pivoting on the switches Clawitzer often forces. Tera Dragon flips Clawitzer's Grass and Electric weaknesses while also boosting Dragon Pulse to terrifying levels, most notably 2HKOing Venusaur.

Clawitzer's incredible wallbreaking prowess makes it great on balanced and bulky offense teams. Pivots such as Mesprit, Whimsicott, and Magneton can provide Clawitzer entry on passive walls, such as Orthworm, Weezing, and Sandslash. Clawitzer struggles against opposing Grass- and Electric-types, such as Whimsicott, Magneton, and Jolteon; as such, it appreciates teammates that it can fall back on against these threats. Weezing, Venusaur, and Muk deal with Grass-types, while Sandslash, Camerupt, and Whishcash can handle Electric-types. Fire-types like Charizard and Pyroar in particular make for good teammates, as they can deal with Grass-types for Clawitzer; in return, while it can wear down or remove their checks, such as Naclstack and Regirock. Walls like Regirock, Sandslash and Orthworm can help Clawitzer against faster attackers while providing further utillity such as entry hazards. Special setup sweepers such as Calm Mind Jolteon and Nasty Plot Mesprit appreciate Clawitzer weakening or removing opposing special walls, allowing them to easily clean up the battered teams it leaves behind.

Vikavolt.overview: Vikavolt distinguishes itself as a powerful Sticky Web setter, pressuring Hattrem and entry hazard removers like Sandslash and Lurantis with its STAB and coverage moves. Levitate and a Bug / Electric typing make Vikavolt a surprisingly good tank against common Grass-types like Arboliva, Venusaur, and Virizion. Vikavolt's offensive typing, powerful special attacks, and coverage even let it deter opposing hazard setters like Mesprit, Sandslash, and Orthworm. What's more, Vikavolt's special wallbreaking potential enables it to capitalize on its own Sticky Web. However, Vikavolt's poor Speed is a major hindrance, leaving it easily outsped and picked off once it's weakened. Taunt leads like Froslass and Primeape can prevent Vikavolt from setting up Sticky Web and get their own hazards up. Fire-types like Charizard and Typhlosion are major specially offensive threats that take advantage of Vikavolt's lower special bulk and lack of recovery. Special walls like Muk and Cryogonal can withstand Vikavolt's strong attacks, and the latter can even remove Sticky Web with Rapid Spin. Beyond Sticky Web, Vikavolt tends to compete with other Electric-types like Jolteon, Magneton, and Rotom, which offer more Speed, utility, and defensive value, respectively.
Vikavolt.comments:
Other Options

Tera Electric leaves Vikavolt free of weaknesses and powers up Vikavolt's STAB Electric-type attacks, but it doesn't offer as much defensive utility as the resistances and immunities of Tera types like Ghost and Steel. Tera Dragon helps Vikavolt catch Fire-types like Charizard off guard, and it even improves Vikavolt's matchups against Water-types like Poliwrath while retaining Vikavolt's Grass and Electric resistances. Vikavolt can aim to be a sweeper with Agility and Throat Spray, but it requires multiple turns of setup to be threatening compared to setup sweepers like Minior, Jolteon, and Mesprit. Vikavolt's poor Speed, both before and after Agility, is also a major hindrance to it as a sweeper.

Checks and Counters

Special Walls: Muk and Magneton can tank Vikavolt's attacks, while Cryogonal can remove Vikavolt's Sticky Web.

Fire-types: Charizard, Houndoom, and Typhlosion melt Vikavolt with their Fire-type STAB moves, though they don't switch into its Electric-type attacks safely.

Faster Attackers: Fast wallbreakers like Sneasel and Hisuian Sneasel pressure Vikavolt and can pick it off with just a bit of prior damage. Even attackers with middling Speed like Alolan Exeggutor, Clawitzer, and Hitmontop can blow past Vikavolt.

Sticky Web Lead.description:
Vikavolt is a threatening Sticky Web lead thanks to its offensive typing and massive Special Attack. Volt Switch enables Vikavolt to bring a partner in safely once it's set Sticky Web, and Vikavolt's low Speed makes it more likely that it can bring its teammates in without forcing them to take a hit. Volt Switch also helps break the Focus Sash of a Taunt lead like Primeape or Froslass so one of Vikavolt's teammates can come in and dispose of them. Thunderbolt's advantage over Volt Switch is that it doesn't force Vikavolt to switch out against Pokemon it wants to threaten, such as Primeape, Articuno, and Clawitzer. Energy Ball covers Electric-immune Pokemon like Sandslash, and it nails Regirock. Vikavolt can opt for Thunderbolt over Energy Ball so that it doesn't have to choose between a pivot move and a reliable Electric-type STAB move across matchups against foes like Orthworm, Muk, and Weezing. Additionally, Vikavolt's STAB moves already hit most of the metagame for neutral damage. Tera Ghost turns Vikavolt into a spinblocker, ensuring its Sticky Web stays up against spinners like Sandslash, Cryogonal, and Hitmontop. Alternatively, Tera Steel has defensive synergy with Levitate, makes Vikavolt a good check to Psychic- and Fairy-types like Mesprit and Whimsicott, and turns the tables on Regirock for a key turn. Focus Sash makes Vikavolt a dedicated lead, while Heavy-Duty Boots makes Vikavolt more flexible throughout the game as a Sticky Web setter and pivot.

Vikavolt can feature on hyper offense teams thanks to Sticky Web, but it's a good fit for offense and bulky offense teams as well due to the speed control Sticky Web offers. Wallbreakers like Glastrier, Charizard, and Typhlosion thrive with Sticky Web up, and they also benefit from being brought in safely by Vikavolt's Volt Switch. Slower wallbreakers like Clawitzer, Glastrier, and Arboliva are especially appreciative of Sticky Web compensating for their poor Speed. Setup sweepers with middling Speed like Mesprit and Charizard also benefit from the ability to outspeed more threats. Knock Off users like Primeape, Mesprit, and Sandslash help ensure Heavy-Duty Boots is removed from all foes so that the entire opposing team is subjected to Sticky Web, and the item removal support they offer helps against checks to Vikavolt like Muk and Cryogonal. Ghost-types like Spiritomb and Froslass act as spinblockers to keep up Vikavolt's Sticky Web, while Defiant Pokemon, such as Primeape and Primeape, can deter and punish Defoggers. Spinners like Hitmontop, Cryogonal, and Sandslash help keep opposing entry hazards off the field without removing Vikavolt's Sticky Web. Vikavolt's poor matchups against Fire-types make teammates like Regirock and Muk vital.

Sticky Web Pivot.description:
Vikavolt is an effective wallbreaker, pivot, and Sticky Web setter because of its unique typing and monstrous Special Attack. Energy Ball hits foes like Sandslash and Regirock especially hard; if Vikavolt is using a Modest nature, it consistently 2HKOes Regirock. Heavy-Duty Boots allows Vikavolt to function as a bulky slow pivot more reliably. A Bold nature with full Defense investment can be used to let Vikavolt better take attacks from physical attackers like Primeape, Hitmontop, and Virizion; alternatively, a Modest nature with full Special Attack investment can maximize its damage output at the cost of taking on opposing leads less effectively. Tera Steel is used to provide a resistance to Psychic, which helps it take on Mesprit after it Terastallizes, and flip its weakness to Rock into a resistance, taking Rock Blast and Stone Edge from foes like Regirock and Virizion more comfortably.

Vikavolt, despite being a Sticky Web setter, is a good fit for bulky offense and offensive structures alike. With its good natural bulk and massive power even without EV investment, Vikavolt can reliably come in and pivot a few times each game, using its wallbreaking proficiency to lure in foes like Muk for teammates like Primeape and Hisuian Sneasel. Similarly, setup sweepers like Poliwrath and Mesprit can overcome their relative slowness with Sticky Web support, threatening to clean late-game after one boost. Stealth Rock setters like Regirock pair well with Vikavolt, with the former taking on foes like Sneasel reliably and the latter taking on Fire-types like Charizard and Typhlosion. Vikavolt is a notable VoltTurn partner for Clawitzer and Primeape, as they can lure in Arboliva and Mesprit for it, while it can provide entry points for them against walls like Muk and Muk. Defog deterrents, like Malamar and Primeape, make for excellent teammates, leveraging the Sticky Web set by Vikavolt to become devastating wallbreakers if positioned well.
